title: Tangy Tender Algerian Couscous Tagine
description: A vibrant, pressure‑cooked lamb tagine served over soft white‑flour couscous, bursting with tangy preserved lemons and fragrant spices.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk white flour with water until a sticky dough forms. Cover and let rest 10 minutes.
Heat the olive oil in a pressure cooker over medium‑high heat. Add lamb cubes and sear until browned on all sides (about 3–4 minutes). Transfer to a heat‑proof dish.
In the same cooker, sauté the chopped onion until translucent, then add garlic and ginger and cook 1 minute.
Stir in the mixed spice blend and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add carrots, tomatoes, and preserved lemon slices, stirring to combine.

Pour in the chicken broth, bring to a boil, close the pressure cooker lid, and cook on high pressure for 10 minutes. Release pressure naturally for 5 minutes, then quick‑release the remaining pressure.
While the lamb mixture is cooking, shape the rested flour dough into small couscous pearls by rolling tiny balls onto a parchment‑lined tray and flattening slightly.
After the pressure release, fluff the lamb mixture with a fork, taste, and season with salt and pepper as needed.
Steam the couscous under pressure for 3 minutes at high pressure. Quick‑release the pressure once time is up.
Serve the tender lamb and vegetable mixture over the steamed couscous and garnish with a drizzle of olive oil or fresh parsley if desired.
